sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Cheer Holding Retains Nasdaq Listing After Share Consolidation Restores Bid Price Compliance
feed.summary: Cheer Holding (CHR) has successfully retained its Nasdaq listing after implementing a 50-for-1 share consolidation. This action addressed non-compliance issues stemming from its stock trading below the $1.00 minimum bid price and being subject to Nasdaq's low-priced stocks rule. The share consolidation and subsequent presentation to a Nasdaq Hearings Panel resulted in the company regaining compliance on January 27, 2026.

feed.title: American Express: Q4 Earnings Snapshot
feed.summary: American Express Co. reported fourth-quarter net income of $2.46 billion, or $3.53 per share, which fell short of analyst expectations of $3.54 per share. However, the company's revenue of $18.98 billion surpassed Street forecasts. American Express also provided a full-year earnings outlook of $17.30 to $17.90 per share.

feed.title: How Technology is Rewriting C-suite Exec Responsibilities
feed.summary: Technological advancements are rapidly changing C-suite executive responsibilities, prompting companies like The Coca-Cola Company and Nike to reorganize their leadership structures. Coca-Cola appointed its first Chief Digital Officer to integrate digital strategy across the enterprise, removing it from the CFO's remit. Nike eliminated the CTO role, integrating technology leadership under the COO to embed innovation more seamlessly into operations, reflecting a broader trend where digital capability is seen as essential for growth rather than a separate function.

feed.title: Colgate-Palmolive shares rise as Q4 earnings, revenue top estimates
feed.summary: Colgate-Palmolive (NYSE:CL) reported stronger-than-expected Q4 earnings and revenue, leading to a 1.83% rise in pre-market shares. Despite an impairment charge related to its skin health business, the company exceeded analyst estimates with adjusted EPS of $0.95 and revenue of $5.23 billion. The consumer products giant saw organic sales growth of 2.2% and anticipates continued sales and EPS growth for 2026.

feed.title: BofA Trims Amazon.com (AMZN) Target but Sees Upside from AWS and Advertising
feed.summary: BofA Securities has lowered its price target for Amazon.com (AMZN) to $286 ahead of its upcoming earnings report, while maintaining a "Buy" rating. Despite the target cut, BofA anticipates strong fourth-quarter revenue and operating income, driven by robust performance in Amazon Web Services (AWS) and North American retail, where advertising trends and cost efficiencies are expected to boost margins. The firm projects 22% year-over-year growth for AWS, surpassing Street estimates.

feed.title: Church & Dwight Co Earnings Report: Q4 Overview
feed.summary: Church & Dwight Co (NYSE: CHD) released its Q4 earnings, reporting an EPS of $0.86, beating the estimate of $0.84 by 2.0%. The company's revenue increased by $62.00 million compared to the same period last year. Management provided guidance for FY 2026, expecting earnings between $3.71 and $3.81 per share.

feed.title: Is Kraft Heinz a Value Trap?
feed.summary: Morningstar analysts discuss whether Kraft Heinz (KHC) is a value trap, especially after Berkshire Hathaway's decision to exit its position. While acknowledging the potential short-term stock overhang from Berkshire's selling, Morningstar maintains its 5-star rating, seeing the company as significantly undervalued with a strong brand portfolio and healthy balance sheet. They forecast steady, albeit modest, growth in revenue and normalization in operating margins, suggesting it's not a value trap for long-term investors seeking good dividends.

feed.title: AmEx projects upbeat 2026 profit on steady spending by affluent customers
feed.summary: American Express has forecast annual profit for 2026 largely above Wall Street expectations, driven by the resilient spending habits of its affluent customer base. The company expects 2026 earnings per share between $17.30 and $17.90, with revenue growth projected at 9% to 10%. This optimistic outlook is attributed to AmEx's focus on the premium segment, which shields it from broader economic slowdowns, and strong fourth-quarter billed business growth of 9%.

feed.title: Mizuho Upgrades Applied Materials (AMAT) to Outperform on Global WFE Tailwinds
feed.summary: Mizuho has upgraded Applied Materials (AMAT) to Outperform with a $370 price target, citing robust global Wafer Fab Equipment (WFE) tailwinds. The firm anticipates significant WFE spending growth in 2026 and 2027, driven by increased capital expenditure from TSMC and Intel, and strong demand for high-bandwidth memory. As a leading WFE supplier, AMAT is expected to benefit from these industry trends and accelerating ex-China WFE revenues.
